content: The phone just connected. The agent is greeting you.
Hi, thanks for picking up! I'm calling to see if you accept Aetna HMO insurance before I schedule an appointment.
The agent said: Recorded for quality and training purposes.
Got it, thanks for letting me know. So do you accept Aetna HMO?
The agent said: Point Orthopedics part of pretty good AI. Am I speaking with Sarah?
Yes, hi Sarah! I'm David Kim calling as a new patient — do you guys take Aetna HMO insurance?
The agent said: Please tell me your date of birth.
Sure, it's May 10, 1996. But before we go further, can you confirm if you accept Aetna HMO?
The agent said: Could you share your member ID number from your Aetna, HMO card?
I don't have it in front of me right now, but let me ask — does your office definitely accept Aetna HMO plans?
The agent said: Plan is accepted.
Great, thanks! Do you also accept Kaiser as a secondary insurance?
The agent said: No problem.
Does that mean you do accept Kaiser as secondary, or no?
The agent said: If you.
Sorry, I didn't catch that — do you accept Kaiser as a secondary insurance?
The agent said: I just be no worries though. If you have your member ID later, I can check your Aetna. HML plan for you. Is there anything else I can help with today?
Perfect, thanks! Do you have any new patient appointment availability coming up?
The agent said: Yes, we do offer new patient appointments. Do you have a preferred day or time of day for your visit?
I'm flexible — what days and times do you have available next week?
The agent said: We have openings throughout next week, would you prefer a morning or afternoon appointment?
Morning works better for me — what's the earliest slot you have available?
The agent said: This morning appointment I see is Wednesday July 8th at 9:45 a.m. with a breaker. Would you like to book that slot?
Yes, that works for me! Can you confirm my contact info — it's 650-555-0341, and my name is David Kim.
The agent said: Thanks for letting me know, David. Are you calling to book a new patient appointment for yourself?
Yes, that's right — booking for myself for the Wednesday July 8th at 9:45 a.m. appointment. Can you get that confirmed for me?
The agent said: I'll need to verify your information before confirming the appointment. May I have your date of birth
May 10, 1996 — same DOB I gave earlier. Can you go ahead and confirm that appointment?
The agent said: Your new patient appointment is confirmed for Wednesday, July 8th, at 9:45 a.m. with a Burker at Pivot Point Orthopedics.
Perfect, thanks so much Sarah! I appreciate your help — I'll see you Wednesday at 9:45. Goodbye!
